title: DORA ICT Concentration — Critical Service Dependency Fan-In by Provider

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: CMDB export via Splunk DB Connect, custom sourcetype
spl:
index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:dependency" tier="critical" earliest=-1d
| stats dc(service_id) as dependent_services by provider_id, upstream_ci
| where dependent_services>15
| sort - dependent_services
value: Multiple critical services routing through one provider’s shared edge or identity layer is hidden concentration. Fan-in metrics highlight when too many important business functions share a single third-party control point.
implementation: (1) Normalize `provider_id` to legal entity; (2) refresh dependency graph weekly; (3) cross-check with contractual sub-processors list; (4) alert architecture review board; (5) attach to ICT concentration register annex.
visualization: Table (hotspots), Graph visualization (optional), Single value (max fan-in).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
